---
title: "Evans Copy"
format: html
---


Loading any libraries needed.
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(janitor)
```

Reading in the data.
```{r}
bike_data <- read_csv("../Data/SeoulBikeData.csv", locale = locale(encoding = "Windows-1252"))
```


```{r}
head(bike_data)
tail(bike_data)

# Structure
str(bike_data)

# Dimensions
dim(bike_data)

# Variable names
names(bike_data)
```
```{r}
bike_data <- bike_data |>
  clean_names()

names(bike_data)
```

```{r}
bike_data <- bike_data |>
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(date, format = "%d/%m/%Y"),
    seasons = as.factor(seasons),
    holiday = as.factor(holiday),
    functioning_day = as.factor(functioning_day)
  )

str(bike_data)
```
Checking for missing data.
```{r}
# Number of missing values
colSums(is.na(bike))

# Total missing values
sum(is.na(bike))
```
Oh wow! No missing data.

Now for summary.
```{r}
summary(bike_data)
```



```{r}
#################################################
# Numerical summaries
#################################################

bike %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Rentals = mean(rented_bike_count),
    Median_Rentals = median(rented_bike_count),
    SD_Rentals = sd(rented_bike_count),
    Min_Rentals = min(rented_bike_count),
    Max_Rentals = max(rented_bike_count)
  )

#################################################
# Frequency tables
#################################################

table(bike$seasons)

table(bike$holiday)

table(bike$functioning_day)

#################################################
# Data Manipulation Examples
#################################################

# Average rentals by season
bike %>%
  group_by(seasons) %>%
  summarise(
    Average_Rentals = mean(rented_bike_count),
    Median_Rentals = median(rented_bike_count),
    Count = n()
  )

# Average rentals by hour
bike %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarise(
    Average_Rentals = mean(rented_bike_count)
  )

#################################################
# Graphical Summaries
#################################################

# Histogram
ggplot(bike, aes(rented_bike_count)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30,
                 fill = "steelblue",
                 color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Bike Rentals",
    x = "Number of Bikes Rented",
    y = "Frequency"
  )

# Boxplot by season
ggplot(bike,
       aes(seasons,
           rented_bike_count,
           fill = seasons)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Bike Rentals by Season",
    x = "Season",
    y = "Bike Rentals"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Boxplot by holiday
ggplot(bike,
       aes(holiday,
           rented_bike_count,
           fill = holiday)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Bike Rentals by Holiday Status",
    x = "Holiday",
    y = "Bike Rentals"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Scatterplot
ggplot(bike,
       aes(temperature_c,
           rented_bike_count)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Bike Rentals vs Temperature",
    x = "Temperature (Â°C)",
    y = "Bike Rentals"
  )

# Line plot of average rentals by hour
bike %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarise(avg_rentals = mean(rented_bike_count)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(hour, avg_rentals)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Bike Rentals by Hour",
    x = "Hour",
    y = "Average Rentals"
  )

#################################################
# Correlation of numeric variables
#################################################

numeric_data <- bike %>%
  select(where(is.numeric))

cor(numeric_data)

# Correlation plot
GGally::ggpairs(
  numeric_data %>%
    select(
      rented_bike_count,
      temperature_c,
      humidity,
      wind_speed_m_s,
      visibility_10m,
      rainfall_mm,
      snowfall_cm
    )
)

#################################################
# Example filtering/manipulation
#################################################

# Summer observations only
summer_bikes <- bike %>%
  filter(seasons == "Summer")

# Working days only
working_days <- bike %>%
  filter(functioning_day == "Yes")

# Top 10 busiest hours
bike %>%
  arrange(desc(rented_bike_count)) %>%
  slice(1:10)
```
